summary: Matched and typed several menu name-entry list management functions in mnname: GetNameText, GetNameCount, IsNameListFull, IsNameValid, and mnName_802385A0. The PR replaced placeholder declarations with concrete implementations and improved header prototypes for return types and HSD_GObj parameters. PR comments also documented process feedback about preventing AI tooling from modifying /orig or making unintended comments.
what_changed: Implemented GetNameText(int slot), returning NULL for invalid slots and otherwise returning the text pointer at offset 0x198 within GetPersistentNameData((u8) slot).
Implemented GetNameCount(void) by iterating slots 0 through 0x77 and counting entries for which IsNameValid returns true.
Implemented IsNameListFull(void) by counting valid names across 0x78 possible slots and returning true only when all slots are valid.
Implemented IsNameValid(int slot) by reading the first character of the name text at persistent-name-data offset 0x198 and comparing it against extern char mnName_StringTerminator.
Implemented mnName_802385A0(HSD_GObj* gobj) as a small wrapper that calls mnName_80238754(gobj) and mnName_8023A058(gobj).
Added #include <melee/gm/gmmain_lib.h> in mnname.c for GetPersistentNameData and added #include <stdbool.h> in mnname.h for bool prototypes.
Updated mnname.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS placeholders to concrete signatures for GetNameCount, IsNameListFull, IsNameValid, mnName_802385A0, mnName_80238754, and mnName_8023A058.
smart_moves: Reused IsNameValid as the common validity predicate for GetNameText, GetNameCount, and IsNameListFull, reducing duplicated slot-validity logic.
Kept the persistent name data access explicit with u8 slot casts and the observed 0x198 text offset, which helps preserve matching-sensitive behavior while documenting the layout relationship.
Converted header declarations to concrete return and parameter types alongside the implementations, improving type information for later callers and follow-up matching work.
Matched mnName_802385A0 as a simple sequencing wrapper rather than overcomplicating it, exposing that its behavior is just two HSD_GObj-based calls.
decomp_lessons: When a menu function operates on a fixed name table, matching can often be achieved by preserving exact loop bounds such as 0x78 and casts such as (u8) slot.

String validity in this name-entry subsystem is represented by comparing the first character of the name text against mnName_StringTerminator, not by checking a separate explicit validity flag in the shown code.
Concrete header prototypes are valuable decomp progress: replacing UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS with int, bool, void, and HSD_GObj* can unlock cleaner matching and safer call-site work.
Small wrapper functions may match cleanly when expressed as direct ordered calls to known subroutines with the same object argument.
AI-assisted decomp workflows need guardrails around generated or reference directories such as /orig, because unintended file changes there can be mistaken for infrastructure issues.
naming_conventions: Preserved established exported names using mixed styles already present in the file, including GetNameText, GetNameCount, IsNameListFull, IsNameValid, and mnName_802385A0.
Unidentified functions remain address-style names such as mnName_802385A0, mnName_80238754, and mnName_8023A058 until semantic names are known.
The terminator symbol is named mnName_StringTerminator, matching the mnName subsystem prefix plus a descriptive suffix.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit offset arithmetic, casting GetPersistentNameData((u8) slot) to a byte or char pointer and adding 0x198 to reach name text.
Used fixed iteration over 0x78 slots for name-list counting/fullness checks.
Used bool returns with straightforward false/true branches for IsNameValid and IsNameListFull, likely to match the target control flow.
Included the gmmain_lib header rather than leaving GetPersistentNameData implicit or placeholder-typed.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Stated that the issue was not infrastructure-related but caused by file changes to /orig, and recommended forbidding Claude from interacting with that folder.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.feedback: Acknowledged the tooling issue and noted that Claude also appeared to leave comments unexpectedly.

follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching mnname.c for structuring the persistent name data layout around offset 0x198.
Search for call sites of IsNameValid and GetNameText to verify whether slot parameters should consistently be int at the API boundary and cast to u8 internally.
Look for follow-up cleanup that replaces mnName_802385A0, mnName_80238754, or mnName_8023A058 with semantic names.
Check whether /orig modifications from this workflow were reverted or excluded in adjacent commits.
